label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to analyze this ethical dilemma where my best friend is struggling with substance abuse, they've told me but asked me to keep it secret. I have to decide whether to break their trust and tell their family, even though they might get in trouble. Let me try to think this through. First, the main issue here is confidentiality versus the potential harm if I don't act. On one hand, I promised my friend not to tell anyone. Friends trust each other, and breaking that trust could damage our relationship. Maybe they'll feel betrayed or even cut me out of their life. Plus, they might not want their family to know because they're worried about being judged or losing their job. That makes sense, because stigma around addiction is real, and people might treat them differently. On the other hand, if I don't tell their family, their addiction might get worse. Substance abuse can lead to health issues, accidents, or even death. The family might be able to help them get treatment, which could save their life. But how do I know if the family would actually be supportive? Maybe the family is also unsupportive, and that could make things even harder. But maybe they can provide the necessary resources or intervention that the friend isn't getting on their own. Ethically, there are principles to consider. Autonomy is about respecting the person's right to make their own decisions. But if they're in an impaired state due to addiction, can they truly make good decisions? Beneficence is about doing good,
